text:
KARACHI – Pakistan’s Olympic hero Arshad Nadeem, who won a gold medal for at the Paris Olympics 2024, has announced distributing 100 laptops among students in Karachi.

He made the announcement while addressing a press conference along with Sindh Governor Kamran Tessori. Arshad Nadeem expressed his gratitude for the success he achieved through hard work and the prayers of the entire nation.

He thanked the Sindh Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah, Mayor of Karachi Murtaza Wahab, and the people of Karachi for their respect and encouragement.

He also acknowledged the respect shown by Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz, who visited his village, and expressed his deep appreciation for the love and support from the people of Karachi and Sindh.

Tessori said people gathered at the Governor House to catch a glimpse of the national hero and honoured him with love and respect.

He noted that the Sindh government has announced a reward of Rs50 million while Governor House has collected Rs100 million for him.

Previously, the Sindh governor had announced Rs20 million, a plot of land, and iPhone 15 for Arshad Nadeem over his record breaking victory in Olympics.
